content: The job interview started with the manager asking if I believed in astrology. When I said no, she frowned and asked if I would be comfortable working under a boss who only hired people with compatible star signs. Trying to stay polite, I asked if the job required actual skills, but she insisted that “energy alignment” was the key to success here. Then she pulled out a pendulum and swung it over a stack of resumes, declaring some “unfit” without looking at their qualifications. I finally stood up and said, “I’m here to discuss my experience, not my horoscope,” but she responded by reading my “aura” and telling me I was “too skeptical to fit in.” I thanked her for the “unique” experience and left, deciding that any job demanding a cosmic resume check wasn’t worth my time. It was the worst interview I’ve ever had—not because of tough questions, but because it felt like stepping into a parallel universe where logic didn’t apply. So if you ever face a job interview that feels outlandish, remember: trust your instincts and walk away from anything that demands you abandon common sense.
